heading: Commercial weather data
label: 8532 Commercial weather data
notes: Amendments2019—Subsec. (c)(3). substituted “2019 through 2023” for “2017 through 2020” and inserted “the” before “National”.Pub. L. 115–423
amendments: Amendments2019—Subsec. (c)(3). substituted “2019 through 2023” for “2017 through 2020” and inserted “the” before “National”.Pub. L. 115–423

children.heading: Data and hosted satellite payloads
children.label: a Data and hosted satellite payloads

children.text: the purchase of weather data through contracts with commercial providers; and
the placement of weather satellite instruments on cohosted government or private payloads.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of Commerce, in consultation with the Under Secretary, shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives a strategy to enable the procurement of quality commercial weather data. The strategy shall assess the range of commercial opportunities, including public-private partnerships, for obtaining surface-based, aviation-based, and space-based weather observations. The strategy shall include the expected cost-effectiveness of these opportunities as well as provide a plan for procuring data, including an expected implementation timeline, from these nongovernmental sources, as appropriate.April 18, 2017

children.label: A
children.text: an analysis of financial or other benefits to, and risks associated with, acquiring commercial weather data or services, including through multiyear acquisition approaches;

children.label: i
children.text: how standards will be set to ensure that data is reliable and effective;

children.label: ii
children.text: how data may be acquired through commercial experimental or innovative techniques and then evaluated for integration into operational use;

children.label: iii
children.text: how to guarantee public access to all forecast-critical data to ensure that the United States weather industry and the public continue to have access to information critical to their work; and

children.label: iv
children.text: in accordance with , methods to address potential termination liability or cancellation costs associated with weather data or service contracts; andsection 50503 of title 51

children.label: C
children.text: an identification of any changes needed in the requirements development and approval processes of the Department of Commerce to facilitate effective and efficient implementation of such strategy.

children.heading: Authority for agreements
children.label: 3 Authority for agreements
children.text: The Assistant Administrator for National Environmental Satellite, Data, and Information Service may enter into multiyear agreements necessary to carry out the strategy developed under this subsection.1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “the”.1

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: 1 Criteria
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , the Under Secretary shall publish data and metadata standards and specifications for space-based commercial weather data, including radio occultation data, and, as soon as possible, geostationary hyperspectral sounder data.April 18, 2017

children.heading: Contracts
children.label: A Contracts
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Under Secretary shall, through an open competition, enter into at least one pilot contract with one or more private sector entities capable of providing data that meet the standards and specifications set by the Under Secretary for providing commercial weather data in a manner that allows the Under Secretary to calibrate and evaluate the data for its use in National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration meteorological models.April 18, 2017

children.label: i
children.text: the viability of assimilating the commercially provided data into National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration meteorological models;

children.label: ii
children.text: whether, and by how much, the data add value to weather forecasts; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the accuracy, quality, timeliness, validity, reliability, usability, information technology security, and cost-effectiveness of obtaining commercial weather data from private sector providers.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, there are authorized to be appropriated for procurement, acquisition, and construction at the National Environmental Satellite, Data, and Information Service, $6,000,000 to carry out this subsection.
where appropriate, cost-effective, and feasible, obtain commercial weather data from private sector providers;

as early as possible in the acquisition process for any future National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration meteorological space system, consider whether there is a suitable, cost-effective, commercial capability available or that will be available to meet any or all of the observational requirements by the planned operational date of the system;

if a suitable, cost-effective, commercial capability is or will be available as described in paragraph (2), determine whether it is in the national interest to develop a governmental meteorological space system; and

submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives a report detailing any determination made under paragraphs (2) and (3).

children.heading: Data sharing practices
children.label: e Data sharing practices
children.text: The Under Secretary shall continue to meet the international meteorological agreements into which the Under Secretary has entered, including practices set forth through World Meteorological Organization Resolution 40.
